methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official RTU results including the RTU Code of Academic Integrity PDF, AI-related RTU news/project pages, and RTU journal pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:rtu.lv "academic integrity" "Riga Technical University" "artificial intelligence" OR "AI"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search identified the RTU Code of Academic Integrity PDF, an RTU e-books graduation-thesis guideline PDF with AI-use sections, and RTU journal misconduct/AI pages.
Plain HTTP fetch of www.rtu.lv robots.txt and the Code of Academic Integrity PDF returned a Cloudflare managed challenge with HTTP 403; no access controls were bypassed.
Direct HTTP fetch of the RTU e-books graduation-thesis guideline PDF succeeded with HTTP 200 and application/pdf content.
Direct HTTP fetches of RTU-hosted Scriptus Manet artificial-intelligence and research-misconduct pages succeeded with HTTP 200.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found RTU-controlled AI-use guidance for FCSITE graduation theses and an RTU-hosted journal policy on AI use in manuscripts. The official RTU Code of Academic Integrity PDF was discovered but not used as evidence because direct access returned a Cloudflare challenge and Firecrawl PDF scraping timed out.

artifacts.claimText: RTU FCSITE graduation-thesis guidelines allow artificial-intelligence-generated thesis content only in limited cases and state that use is not allowed for thesis goals, tasks, conclusions, or original solution content.

artifacts.claimText: RTU FCSITE graduation-thesis guidelines give a reference-formatting category for generative artificial intelligence tools and say a screenshot of the artificial-intelligence tool response should be included in the thesis appendices.

artifacts.claimText: The RTU-hosted Scriptus Manet journal policy requires transparent disclosure of AI-tool use in manuscript preparation, prohibits AI tools from generating scholarly content such as ideas, arguments, interpretations, data, or conclusions, and allows auxiliary technical uses such as editing, translation, corpus analysis, and visualization.

citation.citationTitle: Utilization of Artificial Intelligence | Scriptus Manet
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The RTU-hosted Scriptus Manet journal policy states that authors remain accountable for accuracy, originality, integrity, plagiarism, data validity, and research transparency regardless of AI usage.

citation.citationTitle: Utilization of Artificial Intelligence | Scriptus Manet

artifacts.sourceTitle: Formatting and Style Guidelines for Graduation Theses
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Artificial intelligence tools can be used to generate content for the graduation thesis in case: a) it is not related to the skills and knowledge that the student is required to demonstrate ... c) it is authorized or supported by the study program ... The use of such tools is also not allowed for the formulation of the goal, tasks, and conclusions of the thesis.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 11.2, Unreferenced use of content generated by an artificial intelligence tool

artifacts.sourceTitle: Formatting and Style Guidelines for Graduation Theses

artifacts.title: Riga Technical University AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found RTU FCSITE graduation-thesis guidance that limits and requires referencing of AI-generated thesis content, plus RTU-hosted Scriptus Manet journal rules requiring disclosure, prohibiting AI-generated scholarly content, and assigning accountability to authors. No accessible university-wide generative-AI policy for all RTU students or staff was identified in this run.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The FCSITE thesis-guideline evidence is scoped to graduation theses and related study works under that guideline; it should not be treated as a university-wide AI-use rule for every course.
The Scriptus Manet AI policy is journal-specific author guidance and should not be treated as a university-wide student AI policy.
The official RTU Code of Academic Integrity PDF was discovered but direct fetch returned a Cloudflare challenge and Firecrawl PDF scraping timed out; the worker did not bypass access controls.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
